events.title: European Inventor Award 2026 finalists Industry

items.title: Giuseppe Crippa †, Roberto Crippa, Stefano Felici, Riccardo Vettori, Raffaele Vallauri, Flavio Maggioni and team (Italy), finalists in the Industry category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Giuseppe Crippa †, Roberto Crippa, Stefano Felici, Riccardo Vettori, Raffaele Vallauri, Flavio Maggioni and team, nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category Industry. Semiconductor chips are the essential building blocks of digital and digitised products across sectors, from healthcare devices to smart phones. As part of the chip manufacturing process, probe cards are used to test the chips before they are individually packaged into other devices. Each probe card must be adapted for the chip family to be tested. In Italy, the family business, Technoprobe, has developed a method for the rapid, local production of these probe cards, eliminating dependence on foreign supply chains and enabling faster chip prototyping. Copyright: European Patent Office.

items.title: Evangelos Eleftheriou (GR/CH), finalist in the Industry category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Evangelos Eleftheriou and team (GR, CH), are nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category Industry The invention of noise-predictive maximum likelihood (NPML) detection from E. Eleftherious and team (GR, CH), is nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category Industry. NPML models and predicts noise generated by the tight packing of tiny data signals in magnetic storage, allowing the system to separate genuine data signals from distortion with far greater accuracy. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Angie Triantafyllou (SWE/GR), finalist in the Industry category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Angeliki Triantafyllou and team (GR, SE), nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category Industry Successful patented enzymatic method, which produces a smoother, whiter product that foams well in coffee, while improving the protein content over previous methods. Lactose-free, easily digestible drink and the company went on to become an established company. Copyright: European Patent Office.

events.title: European Inventor Award 2026 finalists Non-EPO Countries

items.title: Yu Haijun and Xie Yinghao (China), finalists in the Non EPO category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Xie Yinghao and Yu Haijun and their team at Brunp have developed a lithium-ion battery recycling method, which involves fewer steps and requires fewer resources than previous processes. Their method uses the battery’s own materials, with a self-propagating reaction supplying internal heat, which results in a more efficient and effective recycling of materials including lithium itself. Brunp’s method has since been extended into major international initiatives, most notably a large-scale project in Indonesia. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Emily Morris and Thorsten Stoesser (United States/Germany), finalists in the Non EPO category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Currently, many hydropower systems are passive, meaning they can only use the water that naturally flows through them. To that end, Emily Morris established a company, Emrgy, to develop a technology that actively funnels water toward the turbines to generate hydropower, rather than allowing it to flow around. This modular and scalable system can be distributed all along waterways to increase the flow rate and subsequently the power generation. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Anibal Montalva Rodríguez and Miguel Ángel Fernández Donoso (Chile), finalists in the Non EPO category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Anibal Montalva Rodríguez and Miguel Ángel Fernández Donoso decided to use their experience designing vertical green walls for public spaces to tackle the issue of particulate matter pollution. They developed the Filtrovivo system – smoke is extracted and cooled before being filtered through vertical gardens where the plants can absorb the particulate matter as nutrients. The device, which is not restricted to any particular plants, is adaptable to each space and requires minimal maintenance beyond basic gardening. This process can reduce particulate matter emissions from wood smoke or other fuels (diesel, biomass, coal, etc.) by more than 90%. Copyright: European Patent Office

events.title: European Inventor Award 2026 finalists Research

items.title: Sir Adrian Hill (IE/UK), finalist in the Research category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Traditional malaria vaccines are not very effective, especially for children, because of the parasite's genetic diversity. Sir Adrian Hill and his team developed a vaccine with more of the malaria-specific protein regions that the immune system needs to recognise to respond effectively, while omitting unnecessary components that could divert the immune response. After decades of research, their work has advanced from a laboratory innovation to a scalable public-health intervention in a growing number of African countries, with the WHO formally recommended it for widespread use in October 2023. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Mikko Möttönen (FIN), finalist in the Research category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Quantum technologies harness the unique behaviour of particles at atomic scales to sense, process and transmit information, with applications across health care, finance and industry. At the heart of many quantum systems are quantum bits (qubits), which store and process information using quantum states, which are extremely sensitive to heat and disturbance. To better monitor and record the qubit states, Mikko Möttönen and his research team have developed an ultrasensitive detector, known as a cryogenic bolometer. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Paula Videira (POR), finalist in the Research category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Early cancer detection is crucial to increasing survival rates, however many cancers lack the biomarkers that can reliably distinguish them from healthy tissues with current detection technologies. As part of a multi-institutional research collaboration, Paula Videira and her team focussed on a specific antibody (a type of highly specific immune protein), designed to target a unique glycans (chains of molecules) found on the surface of cancer cells. Videira and her team’s antibody, L2A5, can distinguish cancerous cells with unprecedented precision for glycan-based cancer detection. This research could help usher in the next generation of cancer detection and therapies. Copyright: European Patent Office

events.title: European Inventor Award 2026 finalists SMEs

items.title: Jan Čmelík and team (CZ), finalist in the SMEs category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Jan Čmelík, Ladislav Mares, Miroslav Maly and their team at Elmarco have developed a needle-free electrospinning technology that allows for the high-volume, uniform production of nanofibres. Copyright: European Patent Office

items.title: Przemek Ben Paczek and team (Poland), finalists in the SMEs category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: Przemek Ben Paczek and team (PL), nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category SMEs. Nevomo, the company of Przemek Ben Paczek and team (PL) has developed a magnetic levitation railway retrofit system, which can be used to update existing infrastructure, nominated for the European Inventor Award 2026 in the category SMEs. World's first retrofit solution that uses electromagnetic propulsion with a linear motor to allow existing rail cars to move independently without a locomotive. Copyright: European Patent Office.

items.title: Franck Zal (FRA), finalist in the SMEs category of the European Inventor Award 2026
items.summary: One of the longstanding challenges in transplantation is preserving organs and tissues outside the body, particularly due to the lack of oxygen during transport and storage. Drawing inspiration from nature – more specifically the lugworm – a marine species that has survived for 450 million years, Franck Zal and his team have developed the first universal oxygen-carrying solution for organ preservation. The solution is based on the worm’s extracellular haemoglobin, which safely delivers oxygen during the preservation and transport period, extending the viable storage time for kidney, lung and other transplants. Beyond organ preservation, this invention is being adapted for use in wound care, skin grafting and regenerative medicine. Copyright: European Patent Office.

events.title: EIB commits €3 billion to Airbus to boost Europe’s industrial base and technological edge in aerospace innovation

items.title: EIB commits €3 billion to Airbus to boost Europe’s industrial base and technological edge in aerospace innovation
items.summary: New financing for Airbus’ long-term research and development in France, Germany and Spain helps advance European technological sovereignty and industrial excellence. Investment in cutting-edge technologies supports Airbus’ leadership across commercial aviation, defence and space. First landmark loan signed under the largest corporate envelope ever approved by the EIB

events.title: 172nd European Committee of the Regions Plenary Session

items.title: 172nd European Committee of the Regions Plenary Session
items.summary: Debate Housing Statements by: Irene TINAGLI (S&D/IT), Chair of the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis in the European Union and Member of the European Parliament Nadia CALVIÑO, President of the European Investment Bank (EIB) Borja GIMÉNEZ LARRAZ (EPP/ES), Member of the European Parliament and rapporteur on The housing crisis in the European Union with the aim of proposing solutions for decent, sustainable and affordable housing Marco CORRADI, President of Housing Europe Debate with members Signature of the MoU between the CoR and EIB

events.title: EC archives of the week

items.title: EC archives of the week (21-27/06) Telemessage: "Enlargement of the European Community to Ten Members" (21/06/1971, 55 years ago) Florence European Council (21-22/06/1996, 30 years ago) First World Congress against the death penalty: speech by Robert Badinter, former French Minister for Justice (21/06/2001, 25 years ago) Luxembourg National Day (23/06) United Kingdom European Union membership referendum (23/06/2016, 10 years ago) Corfu European Council, with the signature ceremony of the accession acts to the European Union of Austria, Sweden, and Finland (24-25/06/1994, 32 years ago) Slovenia Statehood Day and Croatia National Day (25/06) First meeting of the Laeken group on the future of Europe with Jean-Luc Dehaene, Jacques Delors, Giuliano Amato, Bronisław Geremek and David Miliband, and chaired by Guy Verhofstadt, Belgian Prime Minister (26/06/2001, 25 years ago) The Hague European Council (26-27/06/1986, 40 years ago) SME Day (27/06) Signature of the partnership agreement between the EC and Denmark and Greenland (27/06/2006, 20 years ago)
